We'll maintain a cache at each waypoint. We'll move the cache forward one waypoint at a time using both sleds.

Algorithm for moving cache from wa_i to wa_{i+1}:
Assume both sleds are at wa_i with full supplies (4). We want to move some supplies to wa_{i+1} and have both sleds end up at wa_{i+1} with full supplies (or at least enough to continue). A simple method: 
1. Both sleds at wa_i with 4 supplies.
2. Both move to wa_{i+1} (3 left).
3. Both store 2 supplies at wa_{i+1} (1 left each).
4. Both move back to wa_i (0 left).
5. Both retrieve 4 supplies at wa_i (if available) -> full.
6. Repeat steps 2-5 until wa_i has no more supplies (or we have moved enough).
7. Then both move to wa_{i+1} (3 left).
8. Both retrieve 1 supply from wa_{i+1} (now 4 each, wa_{i+1} loses 2).
9. Now both at wa_{i+1} with 4 supplies.

This moves the cache forward by one waypoint, but it requires that wa_i has enough supplies for multiple round trips. Each round trip (steps 2-5) moves 4 supplies from wa_i to wa_{i+1} (2 per sled). To move a large cache, we need many round trips.
